Satan has prevailed upon them, so he has caused them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are Satan’s confederates. Look! Indeed, it is Satan’s confederates who are the losers! (19)

Commentary

The Party of Satan!

These verses expose a portion of the hypocrites' conspiracies and introduce them to the Muslims through their specific signs. The mention of this topic after the verses regarding private conversation (Najwa) is perhaps because among those who held private consultations with the Prophet صلي الله عليه وآله, there were hypocritical individuals who used this act as a cover for their plots and to display a false closeness to the Noble Prophet صلي الله عليه وآله. This led the Quran to address the matter as a general principle.

First, It says: "Have you not seen those who took as allies a people with whom Allah is angry?" (أَ لَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا قَوْماً غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ).

This "people under wrath" is apparently the Jewish people, who are introduced with this same title in Surah Al-Ma'idah, verse 60, where it says regarding the Jews:

قُلْ هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِشَرٍّ مِنْ ذلِكَ مَثُوبَةً عِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَنْ لَعَنَهُ اللَّهُ وَ غَضِبَ عَلَيْهِ... "Say, 'Shall I inform you of [what is] worse than that as retaliation from Allah? [It is that of] those whom Allah has cursed and with whom He became angry...'" Then It adds: "They are neither of you nor of them" (the Jews) (ما هُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَ لا مِنْهُمْ).

They are neither your helpers in hardships and difficulties, nor are they sincere friends to them; rather, they are hypocrites who change their faces every day and appear in a different form every moment.

Of course, this expression does not contradict verse 51 of Surah Al-Ma'idah, which says: وَ مَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ: "And whoever is an ally to them among you—then indeed, he is [one] of them," because the meaning there is that they are considered to be in the same category as your enemies, even if they are not truly part of them.

Continuing the same discourse, It adds: "And they swear to untruth while they know [it]" (وَ يَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَ هُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ).

This is the way and method of the hypocrites: they constantly resort to false oaths to cover their ugly and detestable faces, while their actions are the best indicators of who they truly are.

Then, referring to the painful punishment of these stubborn hypocrites, It says: "Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment" (أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً). And undoubtedly, this punishment is just, "Indeed, evil is what they used to do" (إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ).

Then, to further explain the signs of these hypocrites, It says: "They have taken their oaths as a cover, so they averted [people] from the way of Allah" (اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ) [1]. They swear that they are Muslims and have no goal but reform, while under the cover of this oath, they are busy with all kinds of corruption, sabotage, and conspiracy. In reality, they exploit the sacred name of God to block the path of God. Yes, false oaths are one of the signs of the hypocrites, which, in addition to here, are mentioned among their characteristics in Surah Al-Munafiqun (Surah Al-Munafiqun, verse 2).

And at the end of the verse, It adds: "So for them is a humiliating punishment" (فَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ).

[1]: Junnah (shield/cover) originally comes from the root J-N-N, meaning to cover something. Since a shield covers a person against the enemy's strikes, it is called Junnah, Mijann, or Mijannah.

They wanted to gain prestige for themselves through these false oaths, but Allah subjects them to a humiliating punishment. Previously, He said a "severe punishment" is for them (verse 15 of this Surah) because they severely wound the hearts of the true believers.

It appears that both punishments pertain to the Hereafter. Since they are mentioned with two different descriptions (humiliating and severe), it is not a repetition, as describing punishment with these two attributes in the Holy Quran usually refers to the punishments of the Hereafter—though some commentators have suggested the first might relate to this world or the punishment of the grave, and the second to the punishment of the Hereafter.

Since hypocrites often rely on their wealth and children (economic capital and human resources) to solve their problems, the Quran says in the next verse: "Their wealth and their children will not avail them against Allah at all" (لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَ لا أَوْلادُهُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئاً) [1]. Rather, this very wealth becomes a collar of curse around their necks and a source of their painful punishment, as we read in Surah Al-Imran, verse 180: سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ("They will be collared by what they hoarded on the Day of Resurrection"). Likewise, their misguided children will cause them distress, and if there are believers and righteous people among them, they will disassociate themselves from the hypocrites. Yes, the Day of Resurrection is a day when there is no refuge but Allah, and everything other than Him becomes ineffective, as we read in Surah Al-Baqarah, verse 166: وَ تَقَطَّعَتْ بِهِمُ الْأَسْبابُ ("And all means [of relationship] will be cut off from them").

And again, at the end of the verse, He threatens them with this sentence: "Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally" (أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ).

Thus, He sometimes describes their punishment as "severe," sometimes as "humiliating," and sometimes as eternal; each of these descriptions corresponds to one of their characteristics or actions.

Amazingly, these hypocrites do not abandon their hypocrisy even on the Day of Resurrection, as stated in the next verse: "On the Day Allah will resurrect them all, and they will swear to Him as they swear to you" (يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ) [1]. The Resurrection is the manifestation of human actions and dispositions in this world. Since the hypocrites carried this disposition with them to the grave and the Barzakh, it manifests on the scene of Resurrection as well. Despite knowing that Allah is the Knower of the Unseen and that nothing is hidden from Him, they still swear false oaths out of past habit. Of course, this does not contradict their confession of sins in some stages of that Divine Court of Justice, for the Resurrection has various stations and stops, and in each one, there is a specific program.

Then It adds: "And they think that they are [standing] on something" (وَ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلى‌ شَيْ‌ءٍ).

Of course, this is nothing but a vain delusion and a raw fantasy. However, since they were accustomed in this world to averting dangers and harms from themselves through false oaths and gaining benefits, these ugly ingrained traits take life there as well and manifest themselves.

Finally, the verse ends with this sentence: "Unquestionably, it is they who are the liars" (أَلا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكاذِبُونَ).

This declaration may pertain to this world, the Hereafter, or both; thus, the drum of their disgrace is beaten everywhere.

In the final verse under discussion, the ultimate fate of these dark-hearted hypocrites is described as follows: "Satan has mastered them and has made them forget the remembrance of Allah" (اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الشَّيْطانُ فَأَنْساهُمْ ذِكْرَ اللَّهِ). For this reason, "Those are the party of Satan" (أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطانِ).

"Unquestionably, the party of Satan—they are the losers" (أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطانِ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ). Istahwadha comes from the root Hawdh, which originally refers to the back of a camel's thigh. Since a camel driver strikes the back of the thighs when driving camels quickly, this word has come to mean gaining mastery and driving rapidly.

Yes, the lying hypocrites, arrogant in their wealth and status, have no fate other than being completely at the disposal of Satan and his whispers, forgetting Allah entirely. Not only do they deviate, but they also join the ranks of Satan's agents, helpers, supporters, army, and party to mislead others.

The Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام, says regarding the beginning of the occurrence of trials and divisions:

"O people! The beginning of the occurrence of trials are desires that are followed and laws that are innovated, in which the Book of Allah is opposed, and men take other men as masters. If falsehood were presented purely, it would not be hidden from the possessor of intellect. And if truth were presented purely, no disagreement would occur. But a handful is taken from this and a handful from that, and they are mixed; they are brought together. It is then that Satan masters his allies, and those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Allah are saved." [1]

The same expression is seen in the words of Imam Husayn, the Master of Martyrs عليه السلام, in Karbala. When he saw the ranks of the people of Kufa like a dark night and a roaring flood before him, he said: "What evil servants you are! You professed obedience to Allah and faith in the Messenger, but now you have come to kill the progeny of the Prophet صلي الله عليه وآله.

Satan has mastered you and made you forget the remembrance of Allah, the Great.

Then he added: 'Perish you and what you desire! Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return.'" [2]

Regarding the Party of Satan and the Party of Allah, we will discuss them in detail under the upcoming verses, God willing.

[1]: Usul al-Kafi, as quoted in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 5, p. 267. [2]: Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 5, p. 266.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحَآدُّونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْأَذَلِّينَ (٢٠)
Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle—they will be among the most abased. (20)
كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا۠ وَرُسُلِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (٢١)
Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles.’ Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (21)
لَّا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ يُوَآدُّونَ مَنْ حَآدَّ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَوْ كَانُوٓا۟ ءَابَآءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَٰنَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَتَبَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ ۖ وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ رَضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حِزْبُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (٢٢)
You will not find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day endearing those who oppose Allah and His Apostle even though they were their own parents, or children, or brothers, or kinsfolk. [For] such, He has written faith into their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. He will admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. They are Allah’s confederates. Look! The confederates of Allah are indeed felicitous! (22)

Commentary

Hizbullah is Victorious!

In the preceding verses, the discussion pertained to the hypocrites and the enemies of God, outlining some of their attributes and signs.

Continuing that discussion, these verses—which are the final verses of Surah Al-Mujadila—present further signs of them and clarify their inevitable fate, which is defeat and destruction.

First, He states: "Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger—those will be among the most debased" (إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللَّهَ وَ رَسُولَهُ أُولئِكَ فِي الْأَذَلِّينَ‌) [1].

The next verse serves as a proof for this meaning, stating: "Allah has decreed: 'I will surely overcome, I and My messengers'" (كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَ رُسُلِي‌). "Why should it not be so, while Allah is Powerful and All-Mighty?" (إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ).

[1]: "Yuhaddun" is derived from the root "muhaddah," meaning "armed or unarmed struggle," or it can mean "prevention" (we provided further explanation regarding this under verse 5 of this same Surah).

To the same extent that Allah is Powerful, His enemies are weak and debased; and if we see the expression "al-adhallin" (the most debased) used in the previous verse, this is the reason.

The expression "kataba" (He has written/decreed) is an emphasis on the certainty of this victory, and the phrase "la-aghlibanna" with the "Lam of emphasis" and the "Nun of heavy emphasis" is a sign of the reinforced nature of this victory, such that it leaves no room for doubt or hesitation for anyone.

This is similar to what appears in verses 171-173 of Surah As-Saffat: وَ لَقَدْ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنا لِعِبادِنَا الْمُرْسَلِينَ- إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ الْمَنْصُورُونَ- وَ إِنَّ جُنْدَنا لَهُمُ الْغالِبُونَ‌: "And Our word has already preceded for Our servants, the messengers, [that] indeed, they would be those given victory, and [that] indeed, Our soldiers will be the victors!" Throughout history, this victory of the Divine messengers has manifested in various forms: in different punishments such as the flood of Nuh, the lightning of 'Ad and Thamud, the devastating earthquakes of the people of Lut, and the like; and in victory in various battles, such as the expeditions of Badr and Hunayn, the Conquest of Makkah, and other expeditions of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله.

Most importantly, it has been in their logical victory over satanic schools of thought and the enemies of truth and justice. From here, the answer to the question of those who say: "If this promise is certain, then why were many of the Divine messengers, the Infallible Imams عليه السلام, and true believers martyred and never achieved [military] victory?" becomes clear.

These objectors have not correctly identified the meaning of "victory." For example, they might imagine that Imam Husayn عليه السلام was defeated in Karbala because he and his companions were martyred, whereas we know that he achieved his ultimate goal, which was the exposure of the Umayyads, the establishment of the school of freedom, and providing a lesson to all the free people of the world. Even now, as the Master of the World's Martyrs (Sayyid al-Shuhada) and the head of the lineage of the free in the human world, he reigns victoriously over the thoughts of a vast segment of humanity [1]. It is also necessary to remind that this same ruling—namely, certain victory—is also established according to Allah's promise regarding the followers of the path of the Prophets and the Awliya (Saints); that is, their victory is also guaranteed by Allah, as we read in verse 51 of Surah Ghafir:

إِنَّا لَنَنْصُرُ رُسُلَنا وَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْأَشْهادُ: "Indeed, We will support Our messengers and those who believe during the life of this world and on the Day when the witnesses will stand (the Day of Resurrection)." Certainly, whoever Allah supports is victorious.

However, it must not be forgotten that this certain promise of Allah is not unconditional. Its condition is faith and the effects of faith; its condition is that they do not allow weakness into themselves, do not fear difficulties, and do not grieve, as He says in verse 139 of Al-Imran: وَ لا تَهِنُوا وَ لا تَحْزَنُوا وَ أَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ‌.

Another condition is that they begin changes within themselves, for Allah does not change the blessings of any people or nation unless a change occurs within them: ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّراً نِعْمَةً أَنْعَمَها عَلى‌ قَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ‌ (Al-Anfal 53).

They must grasp the firm Divine rope, unite their ranks, mobilize their forces, purify their intentions, and be certain that no matter how strong and powerful the enemy is, and no matter how few and weak they may appear, they will ultimately be victorious through Jihad, effort, and reliance on the Lord.

A group of commentators has mentioned a "circumstance of revelation" for the above verse: when a group of Muslims saw the conquest of some settlements in the Hijaz, they said, "Soon Allah will also conquer Rome and Persia for us." The hypocrites said, "Do you imagine Persia and Rome are like some villages of the Hijaz that you conquered?" The above verse was revealed and promised them this victory!

In the final verse under discussion, which is the last verse of Surah Al-Mujadila and one of the most striking verses of the Quran, it warns the believers that combining "love for Allah" and "love for the enemies of Allah" in one heart is impossible. They must choose one of the two; if they are truly believers, they must avoid befriending the enemies of Allah, otherwise, they should not claim to be Muslims. It says: "You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day having affection for those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their fathers or their sons or their brothers or their kindred" (لا تَجِدُ قَوْماً يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَ الْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ يُوادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللَّهَ وَ رَسُولَهُ وَ لَوْ كانُوا آباءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْناءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ‌). Indeed, two contradictory loves cannot fit in one heart, and those who claim both are either weak in faith or hypocrites. Thus, we see that in the Islamic expeditions, a group of the relatives and kin of the Muslims were in the opposing rank; but because they had separated their path from the Divine path and joined the ranks of the enemies of truth, the Muslims fought them and even killed some of them.

Love for fathers, children, brothers, and relatives is very good and is a sign of living human emotions; however, when this love stands in opposition to the love of Allah, it loses its value.

Of course, the individuals a person cares for are not limited to these four groups mentioned in the verse, but these are the closest people to a person, and by considering them, the status of the rest becomes clear.

Therefore, in the verse under discussion, there is no mention of spouses, wealth, commerce, or beloved homes, whereas in verse 24 of Surah At-Tawbah, all of these are taken into account: "قُلْ إِنْ كانَ آباؤُكُمْ وَ أَبْناؤُكُمْ وَ إِخْوانُكُمْ وَ أَزْواجُكُمْ وَ عَشِيرَتُكُمْ وَ أَمْوالٌ اقْتَرَفْتُمُوها وَ تِجارَةٌ تَخْشَوْنَ كَسادَها وَ مَساكِنُ تَرْضَوْنَها أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ وَ جِهادٍ فِي سَبِيلِهِ فَتَرَبَّصُوا حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ اللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِ وَ اللَّهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ‌:

"Say, [O Muhammad], 'If your fathers, your sons, your brothers, your wives, your relatives, wealth which you have obtained, commerce wherein you fear decline, and dwellings with which you are pleased are more beloved to you than Allah and His Messenger and Jihad in His cause, then wait until Allah executes His command. And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people.'"

Another reason for the omission of other matters in the verse under discussion might be the circumstances of revelation mentioned for the verse, including that Hatib ibn Abi Balta'ah wrote a letter to the people of Makkah warning them that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله might move to conquer Makkah. When this matter was exposed, he excused himself by saying, "I have relatives in Makkah in the clutches of the disbelievers; I wanted to do a favor for the people of Makkah so that my relatives would be safe!" [1]

[1]: Majma' al-Bayan, Vol. 9, p. 255.

Some have also said: the verse was revealed regarding Abdullah ibn Ubayy, who had a believing son. One day he saw the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله drinking water and said, "Leave a little water in the vessel so I may give it to my father, perhaps Allah will purify his heart." When he brought the leftover drinking water of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to him, he refused to drink it and even said a very ugly, offensive, and insulting sentence regarding the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. His son came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and asked for permission to kill his father. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله did not give permission and said: "Be lenient with him (but in your heart, be averse to his actions)."

Then, it proceeds to the great rewards for this group whose hearts are completely possessed by the love of Allah, mentioning five subjects—some in the form of assistance and success (tawfiq), and some in the form of the result and final outcome:

In stating the first and second parts, He says: "Those—He has written faith upon their hearts and supported them with a spirit from Him" (أُولئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمانَ وَ أَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ‌).

It is obvious that this Divine assistance and grace never contradicts the principle of free will and human choice, because the first steps—namely, "abandoning the love of the enemies of Allah"—were taken by them themselves; then Divine assistance comes to them in the form of the "establishment of faith," which is expressed as "writing" (kitabat).

Is this Divine spirit with which Allah supports the believers the strengthening of the foundations of faith, or rational proofs, or the Quran, or the great angel of Allah named Ruh (Spirit)? Various possibilities and interpretations have been mentioned, but combining all of these is also possible. In short, this spirit is a kind of new spiritual life that Allah bestows upon the believers.

And in the third stage, He says: "And He will admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally" (وَ يُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها).

And in the fourth stage, He adds: "Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him" (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَ رَضُوا عَنْهُ‌).

Compared to the material blessings of the Resurrection—the gardens, the houris, and the palaces—this is the greatest spiritual reward given to this group of believers. They feel that Allah is pleased with them, and this pleasure of their Master and Deity—who has accepted them, placed them under His protection, and seated them upon the carpet of His proximity—is the most delightful feeling they experience, and its result is their complete satisfaction with Allah.

Indeed, no blessing reaches the level of this mutual satisfaction, and this is a key to other favors and blessings; for when Allah is pleased with someone, He gives them whatever they request, as He is both Generous and All-Powerful.

What a beautiful expression! He says, "Both Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Allah," meaning their station has risen so high that their name is placed alongside Allah's name, and their satisfaction alongside His satisfaction.

And in the final stage, as a public declaration indicating another blessing and favor, He says: "Those are the party of Allah. Unquestionably, the party of Allah—they are the successful" (أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ اللَّهِ أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ‌).

Not only victory in the other abode and attaining various material and spiritual blessings in the Resurrection, but as also mentioned in the previous verses, they are victorious over their enemies in this world by Divine grace, and at the end of the world, the government of truth and justice will be in their hands.

Key Points

1- The Main Sign of the Party of Allah and the Party of Satan

In two verses of the Holy Quran, "Hizbullah" (the Party of Allah) is mentioned (the verse under discussion and verse 56 of Surah Al-Ma'idah), and in one verse, the "Party of Satan" is mentioned. In both cases where the Party of God is spoken of, the focus is on the issue of "love for the sake of Allah and hatred for the sake of Allah" and the guardianship (Wilayah) of the guardians of truth. In the verse of Surah Al-Ma'idah, after explaining the issue of "Wilayah" and the ruling on the obligation of obeying Allah, obeying the Messenger, and the one who gave Zakat while in the state of Ruku' (the Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام), He says: وَ مَنْ يَتَوَلَّ اللَّهَ وَ رَسُولَهُ وَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ‌.

In the verses under discussion, the focus is also on cutting the bond of friendship with the enemies of Allah. Therefore, the line of Hizbullah is the same as the line of Wilayah and severing from other than Allah, His Prophet, and his successors.

In contrast, when describing the "Party of Satan," which was mentioned in the previous verses of this Surah, He counts their most prominent signs as hypocrisy, enmity with the truth, forgetting the remembrance of Allah, lying, and deception.

It is noteworthy that in one case He says: "Indeed, the party of Allah—they are the victors" (فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ‌) and in another case He says: "Unquestionably, the party of Allah—they are the successful" (أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ‌). Given that "falah" (success/salvation) is also accompanied by victory and overcoming the enemy, both verses return to the same meaning, with the proviso that "falah and salvation" has a deeper concept than "victory and overcoming," as it also specifies the attainment of the goal.

Conversely, He describes the Party of Satan as being in loss, defeat, failure in their plans, and falling short of the destination.

The issue of Wilayah in the specific sense, and love for Allah's sake and hatred for Allah's sake in the general sense, is a matter heavily emphasized in Islamic narrations. To the extent that Salman al-Farsi says to the Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام: "Whenever I came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he would place his hand on my shoulder, point to you, and say: 'O Salman, this man and his party are the successful!'" (يا ابا الحسن ما اطلعت على رسول اللَّه الا ضرب بين كتفى و قال يا سلمان هذا و حزبه هم المفلحون‌) [1]. Regarding the second point, namely general Wilayah, we read in a Hadith from the Noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله:

"The affection of a believer for a believer for the sake of Allah is among the greatest branches of faith." [2] In another Hadith, it is stated that "Allah sent revelation to Musa: 'Have you ever performed an action for Me?' He said: 'Yes, I have prayed for You, fasted, given charity, and remembered You.' He said: 'But prayer is a sign of truth for you, fasting is a shield against the Fire, charity is a shade in the Gathering, and remembrance is light. Which action have you performed for Me, O Musa?!' He said: 'O Allah, guide me in this matter Yourself.' He said: 'Have you ever befriended a friend for My sake? And have you ever befriended an enemy for My sake?' It was then that Musa knew that the best of deeds is love for the sake of Allah and hatred for the sake of Allah!" [1] And in a Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, we read that he said:

"No man's faith in Allah becomes pure until Allah is more beloved to him than his own self, his father, his mother, his child, his family, his wealth, and all of mankind." [2] Narrations in this regard, both on the positive side (loving the friends of Allah) and the negative side (enmity with the enemies of Allah), are numerous, and mentioning all of them would be lengthy. It is better to end this discourse with another meaningful Hadith from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام, who said:

"If you want to know if there is good in you, look into your heart: if it loves the people of obedience to Allah—Mighty and Majestic—and hates the people of His disobedience, then there is good in you, and Allah loves you. But if it hates the people of obedience to Allah and loves the people of His disobedience, there is no good in you, and Allah hates you; and a person is with the one he loves!" [3]

2- The Reward for "Love for Allah's Sake" and "Hatred for Allah's Sake"

As we saw in the above verses, Allah has decreed five great rewards for those who prioritize His love over everything else and allow every affection to be overshadowed by affection for Him—those who love His friends and hate His enemies. He gives three of these rewards to them in this world and two rewards in the Resurrection.

The first blessing in this world is the establishment and stability of their faith; He imprints faith upon their hearts such that the hand of events and the storms of life cannot erase it. Furthermore, He supports and strengthens them with a new spirit, and in the third stage, He places them in His own party and makes them victorious over their enemies.

In the Hereafter, He places the eternal Paradise with all its blessings at their disposal, and in addition to that, He declares His absolute satisfaction with them.

In a Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, we read:

"There is no believer except that his heart has two ears within it: an ear into which the 'Whisperer who withdraws' (al-waswas al-khannas) blows! And an ear into which the Angel blows! So Allah supports the believer with the Angel, and that is His saying: 'And He supported them with a spirit from Him.'" [1] In another Hadith from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding the interpretation of the words of the Noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله who said: "When a man commits adultery, the spirit of faith leaves him," it is stated: "This spirit of faith is the same one that Allah mentioned in the Quran: 'And He supported them with a spirit from Him.'" [2] From the above Hadiths, the breadth of the meaning of the "spirit of faith" and its inclusion of the Angel and the high level of the human spirit becomes clear. Meanwhile, it also shows the reality that with the presence of this stage of the spirit of faith, a person does not become tainted with sins such as adultery, drinking wine, and the like.

O Allah! If You grant us this spirit of faith, You have bestowed the greatest favor upon these weak servants of Yours, and they shall have no more grief. O Lord! Grant us success in loving Your friends and hating Your enemies, and keep us away from loving Your enemies and hating Your friends! O God! You have promised victory to the true believers and counted them as the Party of Allah; grant us permission to enter this party and let Your victory encompass our state (Amin, O Lord of the Worlds).

End of Surah Al-Mujadila Friday, 8th of Sha'ban, 1406 AH
